rows.summary: Ingrid Release. Minor system changes and bug fixes.
detail_lines: When the invincible startup interacts with an opponent's Throw hitbox, a 12f screen freeze now occurs and refunds 1 Drive bar on hit
This does not prevent Burnout from occurring before the meter is gained, but it makes Drive Reversal a more cost-effective defensive tool against throw loops
As an unintended consequence, the screen freeze affects Kimberly's spraycan setups, causing the opponent's Wakeup Drive Reversal to be punishable if she attempts a throw
raw_detail_lines: When the invincible startup interacts with an opponent's Throw hitbox, a 12f screen freeze now occurs and refunds {{clr|DR|1 Drive bar}} on hit
This does not prevent Burnout from occurring before the meter is gained, but it makes {{clr|DR|Drive Reversal}} a more cost-effective defensive tool against throw loops
As an unintended consequence, the screen freeze affects Kimberly's spraycan setups, causing the opponent's {{clr|DR|Wakeup Drive Reversal}} to be punishable if she attempts a throw
rows.text_plain: Wakeup Drive Reversal When the invincible startup interacts with an opponent's Throw hitbox, a 12f screen freeze now occurs and refunds 1 Drive bar on hit This does not prevent Burnout from occurring before the meter is gained, but it makes Drive Reversal a more cost-effective defensive tool against throw loops As an unintended consequence, the screen freeze affects Kimberly's spraycan setups, causing the opponent's Wakeup Drive Reversal to be punishable if she attempts a throw

rows.text_plain: Punish Counter Throws The opponent no longer gains double the usual amount of Super Gauge (no change to attacker's meter gain) This makes Punish Counter Throws a more desirable option to finish a round while building meter without also benefiting the opponent

value_changes.context: eup input buffer window, the buffer timing will be reduced from

detail_lines: If the opponent's Drive Rush freeze overlaps with a downed character's wakeup input buffer window, the buffer timing will be reduced from 10f → 7f
This change was made due to issues caused by the previous patch (Version 2.02) which increased the wakeup input buffer by 3 frames
raw_detail_lines: If the opponent's {{clr|DR|Drive Rush}} freeze overlaps with a downed character's wakeup input buffer window, the buffer timing will be reduced from 10f → 7f
This change was made due to issues caused by the previous patch [[Street_Fighter_6/Version/2.02|(Version 2.02)]] which increased the wakeup input buffer by 3 frames
rows.text_plain: Input Buffer If the opponent's Drive Rush freeze overlaps with a downed character's wakeup input buffer window, the buffer timing will be reduced from 10f → 7f This change was made due to issues caused by the previous patch (Version 2.02) which increased the wakeup input buffer by 3 frames

rows.summary: Alex Release. Balance changes for all characters.
rows.top_line: Drive Rush Overheads: now universally +1 on Block (-3 normally) with enough block pushback to prevent an immediate Throw or low-hitting light chain (e.g. 2LK~2LP)
detail_lines: Only applies to standard overhead command normals that can lead to grounded combos on hit
Frame Data changes affect A.K.I., Akuma, Chun-Li, Elena, Ryu, Sagat, Terry
Pushback changes affect Blanka, C. Viper, E. Honda, Guile, Jamie, JP, Juri, Kimberly, Lily, Luke, M. Bison, Mai, Marisa, Rashid, Sagat, Terry

rows.summary: Alex Release. Balance changes for all characters.
rows.top_line: Drive Rush Screen Freeze: pressing an Up input during the freeze will now buffer a jump after the screen freeze ends
detail_lines: Still subject to move priority, like special moves canceling the prejump frames
Neutral jumps during the freeze will become forward/back jumps if the left/right direction is held after the Up direction
Only applies if the character is in neutral during the screen freeze (will not be buffered on wakeup or during a move's recovery)
rows.raw_top_line: '''{{clr|DR|Drive Rush}} Screen Freeze:''' pressing an Up input during the freeze will now buffer a jump after the screen freeze ends
raw_detail_lines: Still subject to move priority, like special moves canceling the prejump frames
Neutral jumps during the freeze will become forward/back jumps if the left/right direction is held after the Up direction
Only applies if the character is in neutral during the screen freeze (will not be buffered on wakeup or during a move's recovery)
rows.text_plain: Drive Rush Screen Freeze: pressing an Up input during the freeze will now buffer a jump after the screen freeze ends Still subject to move priority, like special moves canceling the prejump frames Neutral jumps during the freeze will become forward/back jumps if the left/right direction is held after the Up direction Only applies if the character is in neutral during the screen freeze (will not be buffered on wakeup or during a move's recovery)

rows.summary: Alex Release. Balance changes for all characters.
rows.top_line: LK Hazanshu (214LK) damage reduced 1000 → 700 and chip damage 250 → 175; active frames increased 2 → 3; recovery reduced 21 → 20 (no change to total duration)
detail_lines: Reduced horizontal range; removed lower body projectile invincibility on frames 7-18; Juggle Limit increased 1 → 5 (best juggle potential of all her meterless specials)
Blockstun increased 14 → 18; Block Advantage -9 → -5; increased pushback on block (safe vs. Burnout and can be spaced safely depending on opponent's 5f punish range)
Hit effect changed from 0 oH → KD +21 (grounded) and gives a very short ground bounce when juggled on airborne opponents
Cancel into SA3 no longer combos due to the knockdown (even with juggled ground bounce); can still be useful for chip damage or for reacting to DI armor

Hit effect changed from {{sf6-adv|E|0 oH}} → KD +21 (grounded) and gives a very short ground bounce when juggled on airborne opponents

rows.summary: Alex Release. Balance changes for all characters.
rows.top_line: Held Kachousen (236[P]) recovery increased 30 → 32 and total 62 → 64; 1st hit blockstun reduced 30 → 28
detail_lines: 1st Hit Block Advantage -1 → -5; Hit Advantage +1 → -1; Perfect Parry Advantage -20 → -22
Can now punish Mai before the bouncing fan connects if used at close range; especially important in corner setups where point blank 236[P] is throw punishable
At ranges where only a 5-frame normal punishes, canceling into a forward-moving special can avoid the falling fan (character-specific)

The blockstun change also increases the blockstring gap between the 1st fan and bouncing fan; normal-size characters can Throw to punish a close range 236[LP], but tall characters (Dee Jay, JP, Marisa, Sagat, Zangief) will still get Counter-hit by the bouncing fan; they can instead input a crouching Medium/Heavy normal so that Mai is in hitstun/blockstun while the bouncing fan connects, preventing her from getting a combo and pushing her away

value_changes.context: Held OD Whirlwind Shot (236[KK]) startup reduced

rows.top_line: Held OD Whirlwind Shot (236[KK]) startup reduced 41 → 38; total animation 76 → 73
detail_lines: Can now be canceled into Arabian Cyclone follow-ups: Wing Stroke (4K) / Rolling Assault (6K) on frame 55 (can buffer the input as early as frame 37)
Roll cancel is -4 oB at point blank, massively + oB from farther; also useful to avoid being punished by a jump-in
3rd hit launches higher when connecting on airborne opponents (easier to juggle follow-ups if opponent lands into the projectile)

3rd hit launches higher when connecting on airborne opponents (easier to juggle follow-ups if opponent lands into the projectile)

rows.summary: Bug fixes and minor adjustments, primarily for Elena, Mai, and Sagat.
rows.target_title: Serenity Stream > LP / LK / MK on Perfect Parry
rows.normalized_target_key: chunli::serenity stream > lp / lk / mk on perfect parry
rows.top_line: Serenity Stream > LP / LK / MK on Perfect Parry: removed some recovery frames immediately before her forced Stance Exit
detail_lines: This reduces the total recovery on PP by 2f (LP) / 5f (LK) / 6f (MK) and affects Perfect Parry disadvantage by the same amount
This was done because despite being in a Punish Counter state, Chun-Li could block in the middle of her recovery animation on these frames.
rows.raw_top_line: Serenity Stream > {{clr|L|LP}} / {{clr|L|LK}} / {{clr|M|MK}} on Perfect Parry: removed some recovery frames immediately before her forced Stance Exit
raw_detail_lines: This reduces the total recovery on PP by 2f {{clr|L|(LP)}} / 5f {{clr|L|(LK)}} / 6f {{clr|M|(MK)}} and affects Perfect Parry disadvantage by the same amount
This was done because despite being in a {{clr|PC|Punish Counter}} state, Chun-Li could block in the middle of her recovery animation on these frames.
rows.text_plain: Serenity Stream > LP / LK / MK on Perfect Parry: removed some recovery frames immediately before her forced Stance Exit This reduces the total recovery on PP by 2f (LP) / 5f (LK) / 6f (MK) and affects Perfect Parry disadvantage by the same amount This was done because despite being in a Punish Counter state, Chun-Li could block in the middle of her recovery animation on these frames.

rows.summary: Elena Release. Balance changes for all characters.
rows.text_plain: Elena released

rows.summary: Elena Release. Balance changes for all characters.
detail_lines: Now requires the correct blocking direction to grant a screen freeze against strikes
Low Attacks require Down Back + Parry (1MPMK), while Mid/High Attacks require Back + Parry (4MPMK)
This does not affect projectiles (but does apply to many pseudo-projectile attacks like Ryu's Hashogeki)
This change makes meaty pressure inherently less risky and reduces the frequency of "incidental parries"
Inputting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" (community-dubbed term) with a different audio/visual cue
The defender is awarded an extra 3000 Drive meter (30% of a bar) over the usual Parry Drive Gain
raw_detail_lines: Now requires the correct blocking direction to grant a screen freeze against strikes
Low Attacks require Down Back + Parry ({{clr|M|1MPMK}}), while Mid/High Attacks require Back + Parry ({{clr|M|4MPMK}})
This does not affect projectiles (but does apply to many pseudo-projectile attacks like Ryu's Hashogeki)
This change makes meaty pressure inherently less risky and reduces the frequency of "incidental parries"
Inputting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" (community-dubbed term) with a different audio/visual cue
The defender is awarded an extra 3000 Drive meter (30% of a bar) over the usual Parry Drive Gain
rows.text_plain: Perfect Parry Now requires the correct blocking direction to grant a screen freeze against strikes Low Attacks require Down Back + Parry (1MPMK), while Mid/High Attacks require Back + Parry (4MPMK) This does not affect projectiles (but does apply to many pseudo-projectile attacks like Ryu's Hashogeki) This change makes meaty pressure inherently less risky and reduces the frequency of "incidental parries" Inputting a Perfect Parry with the wrong block direction results in an "imperfect parry" (community-dubbed term) with a different audio/visual cue The defender is awarded an extra 3000 Drive meter (30% of a bar) over the usual Parry Drive Gain

rows.summary: Elena Release. Balance changes for all characters.
detail_lines: Many 2LKs have faster chain timing and/or reduced pushback to make them more effective in pressure strings
These changes were made due to the new Perfect Parry requirements, ensuring that all characters have a viable way to mix between high/low pressure
raw_detail_lines: Many {{clr|L|2LKs}} have faster chain timing and/or reduced pushback to make them more effective in pressure strings
These changes were made due to the new Perfect Parry requirements, ensuring that all characters have a viable way to mix between high/low pressure
rows.text_plain: Crouching Light Kicks (2LK) Many 2LKs have faster chain timing and/or reduced pushback to make them more effective in pressure strings These changes were made due to the new Perfect Parry requirements, ensuring that all characters have a viable way to mix between high/low pressure

rows.summary: Elena Release. Balance changes for all characters.
detail_lines: Non-cancelable 2MKs that can start combos (e.g. out of Drive Rush) are now all subject to 20% starter scaling just like the cancelable ones
This change was made due to the new Perfect Parry requirements, as it became harder to defend against incoming high/low mixups
Pressure from 2MK > DR Cancel has been weakened by increasing the forward momentum on many 4-frame light normals out of Drive Rush
Since the only way to create a true blockstring is to make the opponent block an immediate 4-frame light, this makes it harder to bait the opponent's throw tech with a shimmy afterwards

Note that the effectiveness of this strategy varies; in some cases, the opponent can delay their Throw Escape long enough to cover both a strike and throw option, but in other cases delaying the Throw Escape even 1 frame lets the shimmy work properly again
This change is also not entirely universal — Lily DR~5LK still allows a shimmy on block, for example
raw_detail_lines: Non-cancelable {{clr|M|2MKs}} that can start combos (e.g. out of {{clr|DR|Drive Rush}}) are now all subject to 20% starter scaling just like the cancelable ones
This change was made due to the new Perfect Parry requirements, as it became harder to defend against incoming high/low mixups
Pressure from {{clr|M|2MK}} > {{clr|DR|DR Cancel}} has been weakened by increasing the forward momentum on many 4-frame light normals out of {{clr|DR|Drive Rush}}
Since the only way to create a true blockstring is to make the opponent block an immediate 4-frame light, this makes it harder to bait the opponent's throw tech with a shimmy afterwards

Note that the effectiveness of this strategy varies; in some cases, the opponent can delay their Throw Escape long enough to cover both a strike and throw option, but in other cases delaying the Throw Escape even 1 frame lets the shimmy work properly again
This change is also not entirely universal — Lily {{clr|DR|DR~}}{{clr|L|5LK}} still allows a shimmy on block, for example
rows.text_plain: Crouching Medium Kicks (2MK) Non-cancelable 2MKs that can start combos (e.g. out of Drive Rush) are now all subject to 20% starter scaling just like the cancelable ones This change was made due to the new Perfect Parry requirements, as it became harder to defend against incoming high/low mixups Pressure from 2MK > DR Cancel has been weakened by increasing the forward momentum on many 4-frame light normals out of Drive Rush Since the only way to create a true blockstring is to make the opponent block an immediate 4-frame light, this makes it harder to bait the opponent's throw tech with a shimmy afterwards Note that the effectiveness of this strategy varies; in some cases, the opponent can delay their Throw Escape long enough to cover both a strike and throw option, but in other cases delaying the Throw Escape even 1 frame lets the shimmy work properly again This change is also not entirely universal — Lily DR~5LK still allows a shimmy on block, for example

rows.summary: Elena Release. Balance changes for all characters.
detail_lines: Now awards 1 Drive Gauge and 1000 Super meter (10% of a bar) to the defender
If both Throws are input simultaneously, it is randomly awarded to one of the players
This change makes it easier to avoid Burnout or reverse the momentum when stuck in difficult defensive pressure sequences like a corner throw loop
raw_detail_lines: Now awards {{clr|DR|1 Drive Gauge}} and {{clr|SA|1000 Super meter}} (10% of a bar) to the defender
If both Throws are input simultaneously, it is randomly awarded to one of the players
This change makes it easier to avoid Burnout or reverse the momentum when stuck in difficult defensive pressure sequences like a corner throw loop
rows.text_plain: Throw Escape / Throw Tech Now awards 1 Drive Gauge and 1000 Super meter (10% of a bar) to the defender If both Throws are input simultaneously, it is randomly awarded to one of the players This change makes it easier to avoid Burnout or reverse the momentum when stuck in difficult defensive pressure sequences like a corner throw loop

rows.summary: Elena Release. Balance changes for all characters.
detail_lines: The first active frame of sweeps will not extend a hurtbox, making them much better as neutral pokes
Sweeps no longer grant a Hard Knockdown on regular hit or Counter-hit, and Counter-hit sweeps no longer give extra Knockdown Advantage
These properties are reserved only for Punish Counter sweeps
Several sweeps have had their knockdown advantage changed to compensate for this reduced utility
These changes do not apply to sweeps that slide along the ground, like Elena/M. Bison 3HK, Blanka 3HP, or Dhalsim 2HK
Dee Jay's 2MK is unaffected (as it was already not a Hard Knockdown)
A.K.I. and Ed have 2HP as their sweeps rather than the standard 2HK
raw_detail_lines: The first active frame of sweeps will not extend a hurtbox, making them much better as neutral pokes
Sweeps no longer grant a Hard Knockdown on regular hit or {{clr|PC|Counter-hit}}, and {{clr|PC|Counter-hit}} sweeps no longer give extra Knockdown Advantage
These properties are reserved only for {{clr|PC|Punish Counter}} sweeps
Several sweeps have had their knockdown advantage changed to compensate for this reduced utility
These changes do not apply to sweeps that slide along the ground, like Elena/M. Bison {{clr|H|3HK}}, Blanka {{clr|H|3HP}}, or Dhalsim {{clr|H|2HK}}
Dee Jay's {{clr|M|2MK}} is unaffected (as it was already not a Hard Knockdown)
A.K.I. and Ed have {{clr|H|2HP}} as their sweeps rather than the standard {{clr|H|2HK}}
rows.text_plain: Sweeps (2HK) The first active frame of sweeps will not extend a hurtbox, making them much better as neutral pokes Sweeps no longer grant a Hard Knockdown on regular hit or Counter-hit, and Counter-hit sweeps no longer give extra Knockdown Advantage These properties are reserved only for Punish Counter sweeps Several sweeps have had their knockdown advantage changed to compensate for this reduced utility These changes do not apply to sweeps that slide along the ground, like Elena/M. Bison 3HK, Blanka 3HP, or Dhalsim 2HK Dee Jay's 2MK is unaffected (as it was already not a Hard Knockdown) A.K.I. and Ed have 2HP as their sweeps rather than the standard 2HK

rows.summary: Elena Release. Balance changes for all characters.
detail_lines: The Auto/Assist button can now be used on aerial normals, granting access to all 6 air attacks
The input for each air normal is character-specific, but in general, air-to-airs are input with j.L / j.M / j.H while strong jump-ins are input with j.A[L] / j.A[M] / j.A[H]
An alternative L Auto Combo can now be input by chaining a light normal into A[2L~L]
For some characters, this gives a new combo route that works more consistently at longer ranges than before, and the ability to chain into it can improve its hitconfirm utility
However, it will always execute the full sequence even on block if the buttons are pressed (no auto-hitconfirms)

An alternative {{clr|L|L Auto Combo}} can now be input by chaining a light normal into A[{{clr|L|2L}}~{{clr|L|L}}]
For some characters, this gives a new combo route that works more consistently at longer ranges than before, and the ability to chain into it can improve its hitconfirm utility
However, it will always execute the full sequence even on block if the buttons are pressed (no auto-hitconfirms)

rows.summary: Elena Release. Balance changes for all characters.
detail_lines: Starting on frame 11, the base rate of Drive Regeneration is doubled
This is on top of the small bonus of 20 Drive per frame from walking forward, and does not affect microwalk combos (e.g. DRC x3 loops)
This makes walking forward for longer periods of time more rewarding as long as the player is not in a Drive Cooldown state
See the Gauges page for details
raw_detail_lines: Starting on frame 11, the base rate of Drive Regeneration is doubled
This is on top of the small bonus of 20 Drive per frame from walking forward, and does not affect microwalk combos (e.g. {{clr|DR|DRC x3}} loops)
This makes walking forward for longer periods of time more rewarding as long as the player is not in a Drive Cooldown state
See the [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Regeneration|Gauges]] page for details
rows.text_plain: Forward Walk Starting on frame 11, the base rate of Drive Regeneration is doubled This is on top of the small bonus of 20 Drive per frame from walking forward, and does not affect microwalk combos (e.g. DRC x3 loops) This makes walking forward for longer periods of time more rewarding as long as the player is not in a Drive Cooldown state See the Gauges page for details

rows.top_line: OD Freeflow Strikes + Punches (236PP~6P~6P) have 20% starter scaling added to all hits (will apply to SA2/SA3 cancel after 1st or 3rd hit)
detail_lines: This scaling does not apply to the 236PP~6P~6P damage itself, since these hits are specifically unscaled in combos
Starter scaling on earlier hits will carry through the final hit (as though 236PP~6P~6P is considered a single attack)
Essentially, this ensures that any Super cancel will be scaled, whether you combo into it or you frame trap with delayed Rekka hits
All of these scaling changes apply regardless of Drink Level

rows.summary: Elena Release. Balance changes for all characters.
rows.top_line: Body hurtbox (head/torso/legs) expanded forward along with throw hurtbox and collision box
detail_lines: Functionally reduces the relative range of all his attacks (opponents can counterpoke Zangief from farther away)
This also makes Zangief more susceptible to throw loops, as the opponent will be closer to his throw hurtbox than before

detail_lines: Canceling a character's prejump frames into a special move can no longer be used to escape normal Throws as a defensive option
Active frames of a normal Throw are extended when they make contact with throw invuln prejump frames, ensuring a jump canceled special will be thrown immediately
This is especially impactful for Zangief and Lily, who have a longer prejump time that could be canceled into a command throw

rows.summary: Minor balance changes for all characters.
detail_lines: After a Perfect Parry, it is no longer possible to cancel the later hits of an attack
This was already true on block, but now applies on hit and armor as well
This change allows characters to punish more attacks with Drive Impact, such as M. Bison's OD Psycho Crusher and Lily's Windclad OD Condor Spire
Video example of how the cancel used to work
Note: this change was temporarily reverted due to a bug, but was re-applied in Version 1.09

rows.summary: Minor balance changes for all characters.
detail_lines: Startup reduced 8f → 6f after Sprint
All medium normals can now combo into both versions naturally
5HP/5MP now frame trap into Arc step, and 5MK/5MP~HP frame trap into OD Arc Step
The faster startup causes the opponent to be juggled slightly higher when canceled from 5MP~HP, allowing for new midscreen juggles:
5MP~HP > cl.236K, juggle SA1
5MP~HP > cl.236KK, juggle SA1 / SA3 / 236LP (cannot cancel into SA3 midscreen)
Kimberly's Punish Counter state changed from after the 2nd hit → after the 1st hit

All medium normals can now combo into both versions naturally

The faster startup causes the opponent to be juggled slightly higher when canceled from {{clr|M|5MP}}~{{clr|H|HP}}, allowing for new midscreen juggles:
{{clr|M|5MP}}~{{clr|H|HP}} > cl.236K, juggle {{clr|SA|SA1}}
{{clr|M|5MP}}~{{clr|H|HP}} > {{clr|OD|cl.236KK}}, juggle {{clr|SA|SA1}} / {{clr|SA|SA3}} / {{clr|L|236LP}} (cannot cancel into {{clr|SA|SA3}} midscreen)
Kimberly's {{clr|PC|Punish Counter}} state changed from after the 2nd hit → after the 1st hit
rows.text_plain: Arc Step (cl.236K/KK) Startup reduced 8f → 6f after Sprint All medium normals can now combo into both versions naturally 5HP/5MP now frame trap into Arc step, and 5MK/5MP~HP frame trap into OD Arc Step The faster startup causes the opponent to be juggled slightly higher when canceled from 5MP~HP, allowing for new midscreen juggles: 5MP~HP > cl.236K, juggle SA1 5MP~HP > cl.236KK, juggle SA1 / SA3 / 236LP (cannot cancel into SA3 midscreen) Kimberly's Punish Counter state changed from after the 2nd hit → after the 1st hit

detail_lines: Air Slasher shortcut ([4]S) will now only come out while still holding Back (4) or Up-Back (7)
The short period where charge time is retained after letting go of the backward input no longer applies except when manually inputting Air Slasher
This prevents overlap issues with Rolling Sobat and Jus Cool, as the Air Slasher would take priority for a short while after releasing charge

This prevents overlap issues with Rolling Sobat and Jus Cool, as the Air Slasher would take priority for a short while after releasing charge
